stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 25 website users. The sample mean is 156.0 ms with a sample standard deviation of 34.5 ms. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 146.9 ms and standard deviation of 23.8 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 156.0, 95% CI = [141.8, 170.2]. Without outliers: mean = 146.9, 95% CI = [136.8, 157.0]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [136.8, 170.2]. Point estimate: 151.4 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [136.8, 170.2].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
